title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants a `Max TTL` column (value `4h`) added to the Kubernetes auth role table in the OpenBao bootstrap doc, since `setup-auth.sh` configures both a 1h initial TTL and a 4h max lifetime but the table only shows the initial TTL — inconsistent with the AppRole table below it which correctly documents both.

code_comments.body:
**Auth table missing max-lifetime column**

The Kubernetes auth role table has a single `TTL` column but `setup-auth.sh` configures both an initial TTL of 1 hour *and* a maximum lifetime of 4 hours on each role. The second bound was added in response to a previous review round to limit token renewal windows.

Without a `Max TTL` column, an operator reading this table would believe ESO tokens can be renewed indefinitely beyond the initial lifetime — which is incorrect. Compare with the AppRole table immediately below, which correctly documents both the initial and maximum lifetimes.

Consider adding a `Max TTL` column with a value of `4h` for all four mounts to keep the reference consistent with the actual `setup-auth.sh` implementation.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>
